title: Dalea oreophila
taxonomy: Dalea oreophila is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. The genus Dalea is a group of shrubs and perennial plants native to the Americas. Dalea oreophila is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Central America. It is a member of the tribe Amorpheae, which is a subfamily of Fabaceae known as the Faboideae. intro: Dalea oreophila, also known as Apache plume, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its delicate white flowers and long, feathery plumes. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties.
